title: Rate Limiting API for High-Traffic Endpoints
statement:
Design a rate limiting API using Express that allows users to create and manage API keys for accessing a high-traffic endpoint (`/api/endpoint`). The API should enforce a maximum of 100 requests per hour per API key. Implement this using the `express-rate-limit` library. Additionally, provide a `/api/stats` endpoint that returns a JSON object containing the total number of requests made to all API keys within the last hour.

Provide the following endpoints:
- `POST /api/keys`: Create a new API key with a specified description.
- `GET /api/keys`: Retrieve all API keys along with their descriptions and the number of requests made within the last hour.
- `DELETE /api/keys/{id}`: Delete an API key by its ID.
- `POST /api/endpoint`: Access the high-traffic endpoint using the provided API key.
- `GET /api/stats`: Retrieve a JSON object containing the total number of requests made to all API keys within the last hour.

**Constraints:**
- Must use the `express-rate-limit` library for rate limiting.
- Must store API keys in memory using an object (or mock database).
- Each API key should have a unique ID and description.

**Visible Test Cases:**
constraints: Must use the `express-rate-limit` library for rate limiting.
Must store API keys in memory using an object (or mock database).

endpoints.description: Create a new API key with a specified description. Request body: { "description": "string" }
Retrieve all API keys along with their descriptions and the number of requests made within the last hour. Query parameters: `limit` (integer), `offset` (integer)
Delete an API key by its ID. Path parameter: `id` (integer)
Access the high-traffic endpoint using the provided API key. Request body: { "apiKey": "string" }
Retrieve a JSON object containing the total number of requests made to all API keys within the last hour.
